D1 Optics L5 Evaluate Reference

The Poincaré Sphere

place every polarization state on a globe where poles are circular and equator linear

Longitude is twice the ellipse orientation ψ; latitude is twice the ellipticity χ. So linear states ring the equator and circular states sit at the poles.

Set the orientation ψ and ellipticity χ to move the state point, and rotate the view to inspect its position. The equator is highlighted; the poles are right- and left-circular.
